mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60d returns in >70% of triggered instances, or the unemployment threshold fails to separate return distributions with p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with positive 20d returns occurring in >60% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production below -0.5% signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and reduced factory output, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of severe industrial production contractions, or the signal loses predictive power during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while core inflation remains above 3%, real rates tighten aggressively, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns dominate >65% during periods of rising rates and elevated inflation, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household income and consumption capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 20d returns, with negative returns occurring in >60% of months showing payroll expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.0% signals an economic slowdown that triggers immediate market repricing of near-term earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in >70% of quarters with sub-1% GDP growth, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and weak downstream demand, which forces corporate margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The supply-demand divergence fails to predict negative returns, with positive 20d returns occurring in >65% of divergent months.
